questions_generated: What are the fundamental principles of memory_consolidation?
What is the history of memory_consolidation?
What are common misconceptions about memory_consolidation?

concepts.text: Memory consolidation has two main phases: synaptic consolidation (minutes to hours, changes at individual synapses, fragile) and systems consolidation (weeks to years, reorganization from hippocampus to cortical networks, stable)
A third process called reconsolidation means previously consolidated memories become unstable again after retrieval and may need updating before re-stabilization
Sleep plays a critical role in systems consolidation — replay of neural patterns during slow-wave sleep transfers memories from hippocampus to neocortex
The spacing effect shows that distributed practice leads to better consolidation than massed practice
